pattern: A fixed-width sliding window tracks the running sum and a multiset of its elements, recording the best sum only while every element in the window stays unique.
hints: Every candidate is a window of exactly k consecutive elements, so slide a window of that fixed size.
Maintain the window sum incrementally and a count of how many distinct values it currently holds.
Add the entering element and drop the leaving one each step; when the window has size k and all counts are one, the window sum is a candidate for the maximum.

edge_inputs.description: no window is fully distinct so answer is zero
k equals one, every single element qualifies
k equals full length and all distinct
duplicate just outside best window must not disqualify it
duplicates force the window to skip past them
full-length window has a repeat, no valid answer

stress.description: large array over a moderate value range so distinct windows are common but not guaranteed
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 50) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [nums, k]
stress.note: narrow range stresses the distinctness bookkeeping
